title: Georgetown RowHouse
subtitle: A narrow infill dwelling read floor by floor.
statement: On a sixteen-foot-wide infill lot, the RowHouse organizes domestic life vertically around a top-lit stair. Living spaces open to a rear court at grade; private rooms stack above behind a restrained street face that completes the block. Read in section, the house becomes a sequence of compressions and releases tuned to the narrow site.

floors.name: Entry & Living
floors.text: A compressed entry releases into a double-height living space that opens fully to the rear court.

floors.name: Kitchen & Dining
floors.text: The middle floor bridges front and back, lit from the stair void above and a slot window to the street.

floors.name: Bedrooms
floors.text: Private rooms tuck under the roof, each borrowing light from the top of the central stair.

model.alt: Massing model of the Georgetown RowHouse
